mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8 percent, labor market slack signals severe economic contraction and collapsing corporate revenues, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or the signal loses predictive power during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which compounds corporate profit growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns during expansion periods, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 250, inflationary pressures force monetary authorities to restrict liquidity, increasing discount rates and triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and industrial production confirms broad-based economic acceleration, reinforcing earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and INDPRO growth signal yields negative or flat 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is dominated by monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate labor market tightening and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and supports positive equity valuations during stable growth regimes.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when unemployment remains above 6 percent, or the signal loses significance during structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal immediate supply-side contraction and falling factory output, which reduces near-term corporate cash flow expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or the predictive relationship breaks down during periods of high inventory drawdowns.
